frameworks.name: trial history bias analysis
frameworks.one_line: Trial-by-trial systematic updating of decision variables in choice tasks

frameworks.name: slow-drift correction (prior method)
frameworks.one_line: A recently proposed correction for slow drifts in decision criterion

frameworks.name: latent-state model-based inference
frameworks.one_line: Model-based disambiguation of systematic updates from random drifts

frameworks.name: signal-detection / decision-criterion framework
frameworks.one_line: Decision criterion treated as the key latent decision variable

core_question: Can the proposed slow-drift correction reliably recover systematic trial-by-trial updates of decision criterion?
key_assumptions: Decision criterion is the key latent decision variable
Systematic updates and random drifts can both shape trial-by-trial decisions
Slow drifts can mimic signatures of systematic updating
manipulations: Applied the correction across a variety of synthetic updating strategies
Compared inference outcomes against ground-truth generative rules
Tested on both simulated and real datasets
key_findings: Correction fails for a wide range of commonly assumed systematic updating strategies
Inference is distorted away from veridical strategies toward a narrow subset
Proposed model-based approach accurately recovers latent drift trajectories from simulated data
Model-based approach also recovers generative systematic updates
interpretations: Existing slow-drift correction is inadequate for inferring systematic update rules
Disambiguating systematic updates from random drifts requires a model-based latent-state approach
